title: quercus × sauteri
intro: Quercus × sauteri is a hybrid plant species, resulting from the cross between Quercus acutissima and Quercus faginea, commonly known as the Sauter's oak or Sauter's white oak. This plant is valued for its rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Quercus × sauteri include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and protect against oxidative stress, making it a valuable herb for promoting overall well-being. Quercus × sauteri can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
